book_claims.mechanism: Absence of lactase enzyme persistence leads to undigested lactose fermenting in the colon; possible links to autoimmune disorders
book_claims.text: Milk consumption is inappropriate for populations with lactose intolerance (e.g., Asian and southern European ancestries) and may cause illness

book_claims.mechanism: Cow's milk proteins may trigger an immune cross-reaction against pancreatic beta cells in genetically susceptible individuals
book_claims.text: Cow's milk consumption in children associated with elevated autoimmunity against pancreatic beta cells and increased risk for type 1 diabetes

book_claims.mechanism: Long-chain saturated fats raise LDL cholesterol and promote neuroinflammation
book_claims.text: Dairy contains high levels of saturated fats associated with increased cardiovascular disease and Alzheimer's disease risk

supplementary_research.finding: High dairy intake not associated with longevity benefit; saturated fat from dairy raises LDL and cardiovascular risk in observational cohorts
TRIGR trial precursor: early cow's milk exposure associated with elevated beta-cell autoantibodies in genetically at-risk infants, supporting book claim on type 1 diabetes

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Well absorbed but book recommends plant sources and goat/sheep dairy instead
Raises LDL; eliminate from CVD treatment diet
Undigested in lactase-deficient individuals; may cause GI inflammation

synergies.note: Cow's milk is a common intolerance source; goat's milk products are the preferred dairy in the Longevity Diet
Goat/sheep milk cheeses preferred over cow's milk cheese for those following the Longevity Diet
Fortified milk is a vitamin D source, though book recommends avoiding cow's milk; fortified plant milks are better alternatives
